items.summary: St Kilda is appealing the nine-match ban given to Lance Collard for uttering a homophobic comment, saying the young forward maintains his innocence.

items.title: Jet fuel shortage could see major flight disruptions in Europe, price hikes and cancellations globally
items.summary: Europe could experience major flight disruptions within weeks as global energy officials warn the continent has "maybe six weeks of jet fuel left", which could lead to further price hikes and cancellations for Australian travellers.

items.title: Former Austria goalkeeper Alexander Manninger killed in Salzburg after car hit by train
items.summary: Former Austria goalkeeper Alexander Manninger, who played for Arsenal and a string of Italian clubs, died on Thursday at the age of 48 after the car he was driving was hit by a train at a crossing near Salzburg.

items.title: Refinery blaze will not bump Australia to stage three of national fuel response
items.summary: Anthony Albanese has cut a trip to Malaysia short to visit Viva Energy Refinery in Geelong, which went up in flames late on Wednesday.

items.title: Mining magnate Andrew Forrest takes Meta to court over scam ads using his likeness
items.summary: Australian billionaire mining magnate Andrew Forrest is asking a US federal court in Silicon Valley to hold Meta accountable for scam ads using his likeness without permission.

items.title: French Open bans cameras in player areas after Melbourne criticism
items.summary: Organisers of the French Open have promised that their players will enjoy camera-free privacy in a space of their own, following criticism the Australian Open copped from top stars this year.

items.title: The journey of Aled Jones from a boy soprano to consummate artist
items.summary: From learning Walking in the Air on the fly to working with Leonard Bernstein, Aled Jones is currently touring Australia and shares some of his most colourful stories.

items.title: Bank warns of significant surge in inflation as oil price spikes — as it happened
items.summary: The Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) expects Australian inflation to surge to 6 per cent, while adding another RBA interest rate hike to its forecast.

items.title: Carlton confirms Elijah Hollands suffered 'mental health episode' against Collingwood

items.title: WA state guardianship and administration to be investigated in parliamentary inquiry
items.summary: The state agencies which control the lives of some of Western Australia's most vulnerable people are set to be scrutinised in a parliamentary inquiry, with public submissions now open.

items.title: Rising costs push regional NSW shops to innovate
items.summary: Owners of stores in rural towns say the landscape has changed since COVID, and higher costs and fewer customers have forced them to innovate.

items.title: Socceroos to play friendly against Switzerland before FIFA World Cup opener

items.title: Friday fuel filter: Refining the week's fuel news so you don't have to
items.summary: We know you have to get where you're going this weekend. ABC News is here to help with a round-up of all the key news on fuel price and supply in Australia.

items.title: BreastScreen SA did not get ethics approval or consent before research, ombudsman finds
items.summary: South Australia's public breast cancer screening service conducted research without ethics approval or consent from almost 100 patients, prompting the service to "unreservedly" apologise.

items.title: Iran briefing with Matthew Doran: 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon begins without Israeli withdrawal

items.title: Coca-Cola suggests it could abandon Queensland Containers for Change scheme amid proposed reforms
items.summary: The Queensland government recently introduced proposed laws to overhaul the governance of the scheme, while also ruling out an increase to the 10-cent refund amount.

items.title: Myanmar's coup leader seeks legitimacy as family wealth surfaces in Thailand

items.title: Family of 13 becomes homeless amid rental shortage, highlighting national crisis
items.summary: Finding a home for a large family in a regional area has left one family camping on the side of the highway in rural New South Wales. Nationally, Indigenous people are over-represented in homelessness statistics.

items.title: Australia on the verge of an aged care AI boom but experts warn of high risks
items.summary: Artificial intelligence-enabled robot companions, personal behaviour monitors and pain management apps could soon be used more widely within the sector.

items.title: Consumers are cutting back on spending amid higher risk of global recession

items.title: Why invasive plants can harm your garden and what you can grow instead

items.summary: The US president says he "had excellent conversations" with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Lebanese President Joseph Aoun.

items.summary: Defence Minister Richard Marles has declared the United States alliance will remain indispensable for Australian national security even while Australia looks to build a more self-reliant defence force.

items.summary: New research suggests that many dog owners are not skilled in recognising pain in their pets.

items.summary: Anthony Albanese says that new powers allowing the federal government to underwrite the delivery of additional fuel cargoes have been used to purchase 100 million litres of diesel.

items.title: Administrators tell Liberty Bell Bay workers to take leave without pay or be sacked

items.summary: Smoky conditions across Greater Sydney are tipped to ease tomorrow as the wind picks up, after firefighters had a productive week burning hazards around Sydney's fringes.

items.title: Man not criminally responsible for bashing and choking elderly grandmother in Sydney hospital

items.summary: Australia's unemployment rate remained at 4.3 per cent in March, but economists say cracks could start appearing in coming months.

items.title: Uncovered ballots prompt new count in seat of Narungga won by One Nation

items.summary: A small group of officers deserted their posts this week in protest over alleged government corruption.

items.title: NSW nurses, midwives awarded major pay rise after decision from Industrial Relations Commission

items.title: Household spending up in March as fuel costs surge, over-65s lead spending growth, CBA data shows
items.summary: Australians spent more in March as the outbreak of the Iran war drove up petrol prices. Older Australians increased their spending at the fastest pace, CBA data shows.

items.title: Refinery blaze may impact Australia's petrol production 'for some time'

items.title: Alex de Minaur loses to Hamad Medjedovic at Barcelona Open
items.summary: Alex de Minaur's uneven season has hit another road bump as Australia's number one succumbed to a shock last-16 loss at the Barcelona Open.

items.summary: Sam Kerr's 75th international goal has helped the Matildas to a surprisingly tough, controversial 2-0 victory over Kenya in the FIFA Series final.

items.title: With diagnosis rates steadily increasing, men need to talk about prostate cancer

items.title: Coalition's proposal to block non-citizens from first homebuyers 5 per cent deposit scheme cops backlash

items.title: South-west Qld councils pitch community insurance mutual after premiums rise by 500 per cent
items.summary: Adam Osborne was quoted a staggering $60,000 for his home insurance this year. With others in the same boat, six Queensland councils are looking at creating their own insurance mutual to drive down premiums.

items.title: Invasive long-spined sea urchin found on Tasmania's west coast sparks fears for marine habitat
items.summary: The discovery on Tasmania's west coast has scientists and fishers alike fearing the spread of a destructive species to another previously untouched part of the state's marine environment.

items.title: Albanese government's frustration rises alongside fuel prices and inflation
